name: Suvan Sharma
title: Syosset High School, Class of 2027
tagline: Bridging the gap between human logic and machine intelligence, one line of code at a time.
intro: Welcome to my corner of the web. Explore my work, interests, and an AI-powered assistant that knows my story.
bio: I am a student at Syosset High School (Class of 2027) with a deep-seated passion for leveraging computer science and robotics to solve real-world challenges. My technical foundation is rooted in high-level academic performance, including a 1540 SAT and a 94.5 GPA, alongside rigorous STEM coursework in AP Computer Science and Physics.

projects.title: NextStep: Life Unblocked (Mobile Application)
projects.description: A cross-platform mobile application built using Flutter designed to assist teenagers, particularly those transitioning out of juvenile programs, in developing essential life skills.

projects.title: Serious Games AI
projects.description: This project aims to develop a high-fidelity Python framework to simulate and analyze the deployment of Self-Driving Ambulances (SDAs). By combining Game-Theoretic Modeling with Machine Learning, the framework will evaluate how autonomous routing affects patient outcomes, urban traffic flow, and moral decision-making in emergency scenarios.

images.alt: MIT Project: Serious Games AI Certificate

hobbies.title: Building Community Resilience
hobbies.description: Beyond the classroom and the concert hall, I am deeply committed to building resilient and compassionate communities.As a long-term volunteer with the Sri Sathya Sai Center, I lead and participate in multifaceted service drives aimed at supporting underserved individuals in the New York area: Food Security: I prepare and coordinate weekly meal deliveries, ensuring reliable access to nourishment for those facing food insecurity. Juvenile Advocacy: Recognizing the unique challenges faced by youth in the justice system, I have organized collections of over 100 sets of clothing for juvenile centers. Supporting Transitions: I personally manage the collection and donation of suitcases for young adults (ages 18–21) transitioning out of state care, providing them with a sense of dignity and stability during a critical life relocation. Seasonal Relief: To combat the harsh New York winters, I assemble and distribute hygiene and winter kits (containing blankets, gloves, and essential care items) and organize holiday toy drives for children in underserved communities.

hobbies.title: Music : Piano and Viola
hobbies.description:
Music has always been more than just a hobby for me; it’s where I learned the value of discipline and the art of perspective. Reaching NYSSMA Level 6 in Piano was my masterclass in technical precision—spending years deconstructing complex scores and refining the 'soloist’s' mindset to perform at the highest pre-college level.

But my five years with the Viola taught me something different. As a violist, you aren't always the melody; you are the harmonic 'glue' that holds an orchestra together. This dual training has given me a unique 'ambidextrous' musicality: the ability to lead as a soloist and the intuition to listen and support as part of an ensemble.

passionProjects.title: DECCA: Achieving the 'Thrive Level'
passionProjects.description:
I played an active role in Syosset DECA’s 2025-2026 campaign, which earned Thrive Level Recognition (one of only ~1,200 chapters globally to do so).
My DECA experience is the bridge between my technical interests (like machine learning) and my social goals. It has taught me that a great idea—whether a tactical routing algorithm or a community food drive—only succeeds when it is backed by a sound strategy, clear communication, and a strong organizational foundation.

passionProjects.title: STEM Classes: Mentoring and Teaching
passionProjects.description:
I believe that the true test of understanding a complex system is the ability to teach it to others. As a STEM mentor, I am dedicated to demystifying technology and inspiring my peers and younger students to explore the intersections of logic, coding, and social impact.

My teaching philosophy centers on "Learning by Doing." Whether I am explaining algorithmic logic or the physics of fluid dynamics, I focus on real-world applications to keep students engaged.

aiAlterEgo.name: Digital You
aiAlterEgo.description: Ask questions about my background, projects, and interests. Answers are based on the information on this site.
